title: bartschella glassii subsp. nominis-dulcis
intro: Bartschella glassii subsp. nominis-dulcis is a plant species, also known as Mexican mountain mint, which belongs to the Lamiaceae family. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in the treatment of digestive issues, including indigestion and diarrhea, due to its ability to reduce inflammation and relax the muscles in the digestive tract. The plant is rich in antioxidants and has anti-inflammatory properties, which may help to alleviate symptoms of various health conditions, such as fever, sore throats, and skin irritations. Herbal preparations of Bartschella glassii subsp. nominis-dulcis include teas and infusions, which can be consumed to reap its medicinal and health benefits.
